procedure_name: Phacoemulsification with foldable hydrophobic acrylic Intra Ocular Lens (IOL)
clinical_indications: Clinically significant cataract causing visual loss enough to warrant surgery (BCVA in affected eye < 6/18 or patient feeling visually handicapped even with BCVA > 6/18). Advanced cataracts with severe visual loss BCVA < 6/60 or worse should be operated on priority.
Clinically significant cataract enough to account for other visually disturbing symptoms such as glare, loss of contrast or polyopia which are bothersome enough for the patient to undergo surgery.
Significant cataract hampering visualization of fundus for examination or treatment of retinal disorders.
Cataract with narrow angle glaucoma where cataract surgery is required to improve control of IOP.

clinical_thresholds.note: not improving with pinhole or glasses

preauth.label: Clinical photograph of the affected part with Proper labelling of Affected Eye whether R or L
Full face photograph of the patient
Other essential investigations- Blood Pressure & Blood Sugar (Fasting, PP and Random)

claim.label: Still image of the patient undergoing the procedure with patient ID and date

ppd_preauth.q: Previous cataract surgery done in the same eye?

cpd_claim.q: Does the patient have indications (documentary evidence, physical eye examination) of cataract i.e. h/o Decrease in vision, progressive change in power of glasses, glare, uniocular polyopia, white pupillary reflex, VA ≤ 6/9, not improving with pinhole or glasses, along with lens opacity?

Do OT notes detail the steps of Cataract surgery performed (Phacoemulsification technique/SICS technique), barcode & details of the implant used (foldable IOL/non-foldable IOL) and was the surgery successful?
Are the documents available to show appropriate post-op care, advise including for follow-up (at 1 day, 1-2 weeks (optional) & 2-4 weeks after cataract surgery)?

common_queries: BCVA not documented or documented above 6/9 threshold without justification.
Previous cataract surgery in the same eye is indicated.
Clinical photograph of affected eye with R/L labelling or full face photograph of the patient is missing or improperly labelled.
Barcode of IOL is missing at claim submission.
Operative notes are missing or do not detail surgical steps, IOL barcode, or implant details, or surgery success is not documented.
Ocular biometry (A scan and keratometry) is missing or incomplete at pre-authorization.

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for Cataract Surgery only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs, symptoms, ophthalmic examination
If vision does not improve with refraction, a clinical assessment must be made if this is purely due to cataract or ocular co-morbidity
If Cataract is absent, do look for other causes of vision impairment and refer as necessary
surgical_options: Small Incison Cataract Surgery (SICS) with PMMA IOL
Phacoemulsification (Phaco) with Indian foldable IOL (as per expertise, feasibility and availability).
Phaco with imported or premium foldable IOL (wherever indicated, as per expertise, availability and feasibility).
ECCE (large incision) with PMMA IOL, if indicated.
additional_information.pre_op_preparation: Topical broad spectrum antibiotics, QID for 1-3 days advisable.
additional_information.post_op_care:
Broad spectrum antibiotics, QID for 1-2 weeks or longer if required.
Topical steroids 4-6 times per day for 2 Weeks then taper over 2-4 weeks.
Follow up: 1 day, 1-2 weeks (optional) & 2-4 weeks after cataract surgery.
Prescription of glasses at 2-4 weeks after cataract surgery.
Refer to higher centre in case of adverse event.
additional_information.fitness_criteria:
General health stable
BP ≤ 150/90mm Hg
Blood sugar (mg/dl) FBS < 140, PPBS <180/RBS < 200
additional_information.comorbidity_management:
Ocular Comorbidity: Explain implications of associated corneal opacity/glaucoma/uveitis/retinal disease/optic nerve disease/amblyopia/squint/uncontrolled systemic disease; Prioritize care according to the severity of the disease and need for treatment; Refer to specialist for consultation/opinion/management.
Systemic Comorbidity (Essential Referral): Ischaemic heart disease (with request for monitored anaesthetic care and decision on withholding anticoagulant/fibrinolytics); Systemic malignancy.
Systemic Comorbidity (Desirable Referral): Hypertension; Diabetes mellitus; Chronic renal disease; Collagen vascular diseases; Thyroid disease.
additional_information.referral_criteria:
Urgent Referral: White cataract, shallow AC, sluggish pupil, sudden vision loss with cataract, bilateral advanced cataract.
Referral to Ophthalmologist (PHC/Primary Level): if BCVA, vision with pinhole ≤ 6/12.
Referral for complications (PHC/Primary Level): Timely referral in case of drop in vision or development of fresh symptoms after last follow up visit for post-operative complications such as VAO/PCO/CME/Corneal decompensation/raised intraocular pressure/uveitis/displaced IOL/delayed endophthalmitis/scleritis/wound dehiscence etc.
quality_assessment_parameters: Date of surgery, procedure name, implanted IOL
Post operative visit date (2-4 weeks post op visit), refractive status.
Positive indicator :BCVA≥ 6/9 at 2-4 weeks or regains full visual potential.
Negative indicator: vision worse than pre-op or unexplained lack of improvement or serious complications (endophthalmitis/irreversible corneal decompensation/dropped nucleus/IOL)
